definitions.description: Create or replace a bounded UTF-8 or base64 file beneath the directory containing the current saved .blend. Parent folders may be created; executable/script/library and .blend targets are blocked.

content.description: UTF-8 text or base64 content. Decoded content is capped at 4 MiB.

create_dirs.description: Create ordinary parent directories. Defaults to true.

overwrite.description: Replace an existing file. Defaults to false.

relative_path.description: Relative output path beneath the saved .blend directory.

definitions.name: write_project_file

definitions.description: Return JSON for Blender workspaces, windows, screens, and UI areas. Use before workspace/view navigation or UI diagnostics.
definitions.name: get_workspace_layout

definitions.description: Return a compact inventory of latest MCP-readable viewport captures, playblasts, inspection renders, render thumbnails, and render jobs. Use when an agent needs to know what visual/render evidence exists and which resource URIs can be read.

include_unavailable.description: Include resource families with no latest artifact yet. Defaults to true.

definitions.name: get_visual_evidence_resources

definitions.description: Switch the active Blender window to a named workspace/tab. Requires an interactive Blender UI and fails soft in background mode.
definitions.name: jump_to_workspace

definitions.description: Set the first 3D viewport to an axis, camera, or user view and optionally frame an object. Requires an interactive Blender UI and fails soft in background mode.

frame_object_name.description: Optional object to frame after changing view.

use_orthographic.description: Use orthographic axis views when possible. Defaults to true.

definitions.name: set_viewport_view

definitions.description: Frame a named object in the first 3D viewport and optionally select it. Requires an interactive Blender UI and fails soft in background mode.

select.description: Select and activate the object before focusing. Defaults to true.

definitions.name: focus_object_in_viewport

definitions.description: Render a small PNG from the scene camera or a named camera, then return metadata plus exact MCP image resource URIs. Use for render evidence, thumbnails, and client-readable still output. Large synchronous renders are guarded; use start_render_job for timeout-safe high-resolution output.

allow_blocking_render.description: Allow a large synchronous still render. Defaults to false; large previews should use start_render_job.

camera_name.description: Optional camera object name. Defaults to active scene camera.

filepath.description: Optional PNG output path. Defaults to the project/session capture cache.

frame.description: Frame to render. Defaults to current frame.

note.description: Short reason stored in thumbnail metadata.

definitions.name: render_scene_thumbnail

definitions.description: Start a long-running render or playblast job in a background Blender process and return immediately with a job id, rough ETA, and polling guidance. Auto quality keeps final renders high quality but defaults playblast/preview/review/draft jobs to low resolution unless quality or resolution is specified. Use for 1080p/4K, high-sample, full-frame-range renders, frame sequences, or MP4 quality checks instead of blocking render tools or draft_script.

camera_name.description: Optional camera object to render from. Timeline camera markers still work when no camera is passed.

output_kind.description: Use frames for PNG sequences with progress counts, or video/mp4 for direct MP4 output.

quality.description: Defaulting profile when resolution/samples are omitted. Auto uses low-res for playblast/preview/review/draft jobs and final-quality defaults otherwise.

resolution_x.description: Output width. Defaults to auto quality profile: 640 for playblast/preview/review jobs, 1920 for final renders.

resolution_y.description: Output height. Defaults to auto quality profile: 360 for playblast/preview/review jobs, 1080 for final renders.

samples.description: Cycles/Eevee render samples. Defaults to auto quality profile: low for playblast/preview/review jobs, higher for final renders.

definitions.name: start_render_job

definitions.description: Poll a background render job for status, frame progress, output paths, latest frame resource URI, log tail, and completion state.
definitions.name: get_render_job_status

definitions.description: Cancel a tracked background render job started by this Blender bridge session.
definitions.name: cancel_render_job

definitions.description: Assemble a completed PNG frame-sequence render job into an MP4 in a background Blender process, then poll get_render_job_status for completion.

allow_partial.description: Allow assembly before every expected frame is present. Defaults to false.

fps.description: Optional output FPS. Defaults to the render job FPS.

output_path.description: Optional MP4 path. Relative paths resolve inside the render job folder.

overwrite.description: Overwrite an existing MP4. Defaults to true.

quality.description: Blender FFmpeg quality. Defaults to HIGH.

definitions.name: assemble_render_job_video

definitions.description: Validate render job frame completeness, MP4 presence/size, and useful output resource URIs before reporting a render as done.

min_video_size_bytes.description: Minimum acceptable MP4 size. Defaults to 1 byte.

require_video.description: Require MP4 output. Defaults to true.

definitions.name: validate_render_job_output

definitions.description: Fetch deeper read-only details for named objects, selected objects, or the active object.

selected_only.description: Inspect current selected objects when object_names is empty

definitions.name: get_object_details

definitions.description: List objects in the current scene with names, types, selection state, visibility, collections, and locations.

type_filter.description: Optional Blender object type such as MESH, CAMERA, LIGHT, EMPTY

definitions.name: list_scene_objects

definitions.description: Fetch read-only scene, object, action, f-curve, and keyframe details for animation planning.

max_keyframes_per_curve.description: Maximum keyframes to show per f-curve

object_names.description: Optional object names whose actions should be inspected

definitions.name: get_animation_details

definitions.description: Build read-only animation-aware scene context that identifies likely edit targets, rig-driven objects, rig control candidates, shape keys, constraints, drivers, NLA, physics/simulation hints, contact surfaces, camera readiness, hardening risk flags, required pre-mutation inspections, and recommended deeper inspection/review tools.

object_names.description: Optional objects to inspect. Defaults to the scene unless selected_only is true.

selected_only.description: Inspect only selected objects when object_names is empty.

definitions.name: get_animation_scene_context

definitions.description: Create a structured animation prompt contract with subjects, action, timing, assumptions, ambiguities, success criteria, and validation checks. Does not mutate the scene.

action.description: Optional explicit primary action if the prompt is ambiguous.

camera.description: Optional camera name or framing requirement.

prompt.description: The user's animation request or concise animation intent to formalize.

style.description: Optional style/read such as playful, heavy, snappy, or cinematic.

subject_names.description: Optional existing Blender object names to treat as animated subjects. Defaults to the current selection.

definitions.name: create_animation_brief

definitions.description: Create a read-only animator-style timing chart from a prompt or animation brief, with key poses, contacts, holds, breakdowns, and spacing notes.
definitions.name: create_timing_chart

definitions.description: Plan the Milestone 7 animation workflow for generation, review, or repair. Returns a brief, animation-aware scene context, timing chart, ordered helper/evaluator/repair tool calls, and explicit draft_script fallback rules. Does not mutate the scene.

findings.description: Optional evaluator findings to plan repair calls.

mode.description: Workflow focus. full includes generation and validation guidance.

prompt.description: The user's animation generation, review, or repair request.

subject_names.description: Optional existing Blender object names to treat as animated subjects.

definitions.name: plan_animation_workflow

definitions.description: Execute the Milestone 7 helper-backed animation workflow for common requests, then run structured review and optional bounded repair. May leave live preview changes pending for commit/revert.

apply_generation.description: Whether to execute allowlisted generation helpers from the workflow. Defaults to true.

apply_repairs.description: Whether to execute bounded repair operations from evaluator findings. Defaults to false.

capture_playblast.description: Whether to request sampled viewport frames during review. Defaults to false for headless-safe use.

prompt.description: The user's animation generation, review, or repair request.

run_review.description: Whether to run structured evaluator review after generation. Defaults to true.

definitions.name: run_animation_workflow

definitions.description: One-input animation entry point for MCP clients. Routes the prompt through the Milestone 7 planner/runner workflow before any draft_script fallback.

prompt.description: The user's animation generation, review, or repair request.

definitions.name: run_animation_task

definitions.description: Read-only analysis of sampled keyed location motion arcs for selected or named objects.
definitions.name: analyze_motion_arcs

definitions.description: Read-only analysis of transform key spacing, segment distances, and interpolation choices.
definitions.name: analyze_fcurve_spacing

definitions.description: Read-only analysis of keyed pose count, transform readability, and detected holds.
definitions.name: analyze_pose_clarity

definitions.description: Evaluate keyed animation against core animation principles and the prompt contract. Returns structured findings that can feed repair.
definitions.name: analyze_animation_principles

definitions.description: Sample selected or named object transforms across an animation range for objective review.
definitions.name: sample_animation_state

definitions.description: Detect sliding while animated object bounds are near a contact plane.
definitions.name: analyze_contact_sliding

definitions.description: Detect sampled world bounding-box intersections between animated objects.
definitions.name: analyze_collision_penetration

definitions.description: Check sampled subject centers against support-surface footprints for balance, weight, and contact plausibility.
definitions.name: analyze_center_of_mass

definitions.description: Check whether animated subjects stay inside a camera-safe region.
definitions.name: analyze_camera_framing

definitions.description: Check sampled speed and acceleration for physically implausible spikes in Blender units.
definitions.name: analyze_motion_physics

definitions.description: Compare current animation samples and framing against a structured animation brief or prompt.
definitions.name: compare_animation_to_brief

definitions.description: Review playblast visual frame evidence, compact pixel motion evidence, and current animation state against a prompt contract. Returns structured findings and repair_operations with executable tool_call name/input pairs.
definitions.name: review_playblast_against_brief

definitions.description: Review diagnostic object render evidence against a prompt contract. Returns visual-detail findings and repair_operations, including focused recapture calls when views or image evidence are missing.
definitions.name: review_inspection_renders_against_brief

definitions.description: Turn structured animation findings into focused repair operations with suggested helper tool arguments and executable tool_call name/input pairs. Does not mutate the scene.
definitions.name: repair_animation_from_findings

definitions.description: Apply a bounded set of animation repair_operations through safe helper tools, optionally recapture playblast evidence, and re-run review_playblast_against_brief. May leave preview changes pending for commit/revert.

allowed_tools.description: Optional allowlist of repair helper tool names for this loop.

apply_mutating_repairs.description: Whether to execute preview-safe mutating repair helpers. Defaults to true.

max_operations.description: Maximum helper operations to execute. Defaults to 4.

recapture_after_mutation.description: Whether to request a fresh playblast after mutating repairs before final review. Defaults to true.

definitions.name: run_animation_repair_loop

extend.description: Keep existing selection instead of replacing it

definitions.name: set_current_frame

location.description: Optional absolute [x, y, z] location in Blender units

rotation.description: Optional absolute Euler rotation in radians

definitions.name: bake_maps

Use this for procedural stone, marble, wood, cellular, fabric, or musgrave-style texture requests before custom shader-node Python.

definitions.description: Read-only UV layout quality gate for missing UVs, near-zero area, possible overlapping face bounds, seam counts, layout stats, and unapplied-scale warnings. max_overlap_pairs=0 disables the overlap heuristic.

definitions.description: Add a valid Geometry Nodes modifier and starter node group to selected mesh objects. Templates include passthrough, transform, join geometry, set position, and subdivide mesh. Applies immediately with preview revert support.

definitions.name: apply_vehicle_refinement_template

definitions.description: Apply a bounded product presentation kit around a target: material polish, smoothing/bevels, optional studio stage, dimension callouts, and optional turntable. Applies immediately with preview revert support.
definitions.name: apply_product_refinement_template

definitions.description: Apply a bounded character presentation kit around a target mesh: body polish, simple head/neck/eyes, shoulder marker, and optional gesture guides. Applies immediately with preview revert support.
definitions.name: apply_character_refinement_template

definitions.description: Create a production-style product presentation stage around a target object: floor, backdrop, key/fill/rim area lights, target empty, and optional camera. Applies immediately with preview revert support.
definitions.name: create_studio_product_stage

definitions.description: Add non-destructive dimension/ruler callouts around a target object's bounds for width, depth, and height. Applies immediately with preview revert support.
definitions.name: add_dimension_callouts

definitions.description: Create a bounded production lighting preset around a target object. Presets include product_softbox, dramatic_rim, and gallery_even. Applies immediately with preview revert support.
definitions.name: apply_lighting_preset

definitions.description: Create a bounded production material palette, optional swatch cubes, and optional assignment to selected mesh objects. Applies immediately with preview revert support.
definitions.name: create_material_palette

definitions.description: Create a product turntable setup around a target: optional stage, rotating target animation, and orbit camera. Applies immediately with preview revert support.
definitions.name: create_product_turntable_setup

definitions.description: Create a bounded look-dev review setup around a target: optional stage, turntable animation, render engine/quality controls, inspection still capture, and artifact validation. Applies scene changes with preview revert support while render evidence remains as MCP resources.
definitions.name: create_lookdev_turntable_review

definitions.description: Organize imported asset objects, fill missing mesh materials without overwriting imported materials, create a bounded studio/turntable presentation setup, and leave the result in preview. Use after an external asset import job completes.
definitions.name: prepare_imported_asset_presentation

definitions.description: Link scene or selected objects into production-oriented type collections without deleting original links. Applies immediately with preview revert support.
definitions.name: organize_scene_for_production

definitions.description: Add a Track To constraint from selected object(s) to a target object. Useful for cameras/lights looking at a target. Applies immediately with preview revert support.
definitions.name: add_track_to_constraint

definitions.description: Add a light to the current Blender scene. Applies immediately with preview revert support.
definitions.name: add_light

definitions.description: Add a camera and make it the active scene camera. Applies immediately with preview revert support.
definitions.name: add_camera

definitions.description: Set the scene timeline frame range, current frame, and optionally FPS. Applies immediately with preview revert support.
definitions.name: set_scene_frame_range

definitions.description: Set an existing camera object as the active scene camera. Applies immediately with preview revert support.
definitions.name: set_active_camera

definitions.description: Create simple transform keyframes for selected objects using a preview action. Applies immediately with preview revert support.
definitions.name: animate_selected_transform

definitions.description: Create a camera orbit rig around a target object and keyframe it over a frame range. Applies immediately with preview revert support.
definitions.name: create_camera_orbit

definitions.description: Search cached/official Blender docs. Use before unfamiliar or version-sensitive APIs.
definitions.name: search_blender_docs

definitions.description: List Poly Haven catalog categories for HDRIs, textures, and models. Returns metadata only; it does not download or import assets.

timeout.description: HTTP timeout in seconds. Defaults to 15.

definitions.name: list_poly_haven_categories

definitions.description: Search Poly Haven's CC0 asset catalog and return source URLs plus API file URLs. Returns metadata only; it does not download or import assets.

limit.description: Maximum assets to return. Defaults to 20, max 50.

query.description: Optional local text filter over returned asset names, ids, and categories.

timeout.description: HTTP timeout in seconds. Defaults to 15.

definitions.name: search_poly_haven_assets

definitions.description: Fetch and summarize the available Poly Haven files for one asset, including resolutions, formats, sizes, hashes, and include dependencies. Does not download or import.
definitions.name: inspect_poly_haven_asset_files

definitions.description: Synchronous fallback: download and cache selected Poly Haven HDRI, texture, or model files with checksum validation. Does not mutate the Blender scene. For normal client workflows, prefer start_external_asset_download and poll get_external_asset_job_status.
definitions.name: download_poly_haven_asset

definitions.description: Synchronous fallback: download/cache and import a Poly Haven asset into Blender preview. HDRIs create a world, textures create/assign a material, and models use Blender importers. For normal client workflows, use start_external_asset_download, poll get_external_asset_job_status, then start_external_asset_import_job.

allow_duplicate.description: Allow another overlapping copy when this asset is already present.

definitions.name: import_poly_haven_asset

definitions.description: Search Sketchfab's public model catalog and return viewer, author, license, thumbnail, and downloadability metadata. Returns metadata only; it does not download or import assets.

downloadable.description: Filter for downloadable models. Defaults to true.

limit.description: Maximum models to return. Defaults to 20, max 50.

timeout.description: HTTP timeout in seconds. Defaults to 15.

definitions.name: search_sketchfab_models

definitions.description: Synchronous fallback: use a Sketchfab API token to fetch temporary GLTF download info, cache the archive, and extract an importable model file. Does not mutate the Blender scene. For normal client workflows, prefer start_external_asset_download and poll get_external_asset_job_status.

api_token.description: Optional per-call Sketchfab API token. Redacted in audit logs.

token_env_var.description: Sketchfab API token environment variable to read when api_token is omitted. Defaults to SKETCHFAB_API_TOKEN and falls back to the bridge-specific name.

definitions.name: download_sketchfab_model

definitions.description: Synchronous fallback: use a Sketchfab API token to cache a downloadable model archive and import the extracted GLTF/GLB file into Blender preview. For normal client workflows, use start_external_asset_download, poll get_external_asset_job_status, then start_external_asset_import_job.

allow_duplicate.description: Allow another overlapping copy when this asset is already present.

api_token.description: Optional per-call Sketchfab API token. Redacted in audit logs.

token_env_var.description: Sketchfab API token environment variable to read when api_token is omitted. Defaults to SKETCHFAB_API_TOKEN and falls back to the bridge-specific name.

definitions.name: import_sketchfab_model

definitions.description: Default client path: start an asynchronous external asset download/cache job for Poly Haven or Sketchfab. Use this for any normal asset download/cache or import request. Returns immediately with a job id; poll get_external_asset_job_status until completed or failed, then queue scene import with start_external_asset_import_job.

api_token.description: Optional per-call Sketchfab API token. Redacted in audit logs and not persisted to job metadata.

token_env_var.description: Sketchfab API token environment variable to read when api_token is omitted.

definitions.name: start_external_asset_download

definitions.description: Poll an asynchronous external asset download/cache job for status, progress, cached manifest path, and import readiness. When completed and the user wants scene import, call start_external_asset_import_job.
definitions.name: get_external_asset_job_status

definitions.description: Cancel an asynchronous external asset download/cache job. Subprocess jobs are terminated; in-process compatibility jobs stop cooperatively.
definitions.name: cancel_external_asset_job

definitions.description: Synchronous fallback: import a completed external asset download/cache job result into Blender preview using the cached manifest. For normal client workflows, prefer start_external_asset_import_job and poll get_external_asset_import_job_status.
definitions.name: import_external_asset_job_result

definitions.description: Default client path after a completed asset download/cache job: queue Blender main-thread import and return immediately with a pollable import job id. Poll get_external_asset_import_job_status until completed or failed.

manifest_path.description: Optional cached asset manifest path when no source_job_id is supplied.

source_job_id.description: Completed external asset download/cache job id.

definitions.name: start_external_asset_import_job

definitions.description: Poll a queued external asset import job for queued/running/completed/failed/cancelled status and import result details. Use this after start_external_asset_import_job before reporting import success.
definitions.name: get_external_asset_import_job_status

definitions.description: Cancel a queued external asset import job. Imports already running on Blender's main thread cannot be interrupted safely.
definitions.name: cancel_external_asset_import_job

definitions.description: Delete completed, failed, or cancelled external asset job metadata/log files. Dry-run by default.
definitions.name: delete_external_asset_job

definitions.description: Report cached/imported external assets, providers, licenses, source URLs, files, and imported Blender data-block names.
definitions.name: get_external_asset_cache_diagnostics

definitions.description: Preview or delete old/excess external asset cache directories by age or total size. Dry-run by default.
definitions.name: prune_external_asset_cache

definitions.description: Run Blender Python immediately with the same process permissions as Blender's Run Script command when Blender-side session script trust is active. Filesystem, network, subprocess, project-file, and Blender API access are allowed. When trust is off the request is refused without staging a pending script. Prefer bounded helpers when they provide better recovery or progress reporting.

code.description: Complete Blender Python script to run under active session trust

expected_changes.description: Visible scene/data changes the user should expect if they approve it

risk_level.description: Risk estimate based on scope, destructiveness, and API uncertainty

target_objects.description: Object or datablock names the script intends to touch

definitions.name: draft_script

definitions.description: Compatibility alias for draft_script. Under active Blender-side session trust it runs generated Python with the same process permissions as Blender's Run Script command; otherwise it refuses.

approval_summary.description: Legacy compatibility field retained as advisory context; it does not authorize execution

capabilities.description: Legacy capability declaration retained as advisory context; active session trust grants full manual-script permissions.

code.description: Complete Blender Python script to run under active session trust

declared_paths.description: Files, directories, cache locations, or .blend paths the script may read/write/open/save

declared_urls.description: Network URLs, API endpoints, providers, or asset sources the script may contact

destructive_actions.description: Expected overwrite, open, delete, import, save, or discard operations. Use an empty list only when none are expected.

expected_changes.description: Visible scene, file, asset-cache, or project-file changes expected when it runs

intent.description: Plain-language reason for the privileged script

risk_level.description: Risk estimate based on file/network/project impact. Defaults to high.

script_kind.description: Legacy workflow classification retained for client compatibility and audit context.

target_objects.description: Object or datablock names the script intends to touch

definitions.name: draft_privileged_script

definitions.description: Commit the current live preview transaction.
definitions.name: commit_preview

definitions.description: Revert the full live preview transaction, or remove only the latest isolated imported-asset step.

scope.description: Defaults to all. last_step is accepted only for an isolated creation step such as an asset import.

definitions.name: revert_preview

contract.description: Return a compact context bundle for the active Blender scene

specs.description: Inspect the current Blender scene and selected objects. Use before acting if context may be stale.

include_visual.description: Whether viewport image context was requested

specs.name: inspect_scene

contract.description: Capture the current viewport/window and return visual metadata plus a local artifact path

specs.description: Capture the current Blender viewport/window and return visual context metadata plus a local artifact path. Use when an external agent needs explicit screenshot evidence or MCP-readable image resources.

max_bytes.description: Maximum PNG bytes to keep after resizing/compression. Defaults to the add-on preference.

specs.name: capture_viewport

contract.description: Capture sampled low-resolution viewport frames across an animation range for visual review
contract.duration_hint: Synchronous viewport capture; rough estimate is about 1 second per sampled frame, with the default 12 low-resolution preview frames often around 10-20 seconds on large scenes.

shading.description: Optional viewport shading for the capture. Defaults to the current viewport; use MATERIAL or RENDERED to review materials and lighting.

timeout_recovery.message: If the MCP client times out, the playblast may still finish. Wait, check bridge status, then read latest playblast metadata or audit logs before recapturing.

specs.description: Capture sampled low-resolution viewport frames across an animation range and return playblast metadata plus MCP frame resource URIs for visual animation review. Defaults to preview quality capped at 640x360; request quality/max_width/max_height only when higher fidelity is needed. Requires an interactive Blender window and fails soft in background mode. This is synchronous and roughly 1 second per sampled frame; if it times out, wait and check blender_bridge_status/latest playblast metadata before recapturing.

brief.description: Short animation intent or prompt contract to store with the playblast metadata.

max_bytes.description: Maximum PNG bytes per sampled frame after resizing/compression.

max_frames.description: Maximum sampled frames to capture from the animation range. Defaults to 12.

max_height.description: Optional maximum stored frame height. Overrides the quality preset.

max_width.description: Optional maximum stored frame width. Overrides the quality preset.

quality.description: Frame-size preset. Defaults to preview/low at 640x360; source/original keeps viewport dimensions.

shading.description: Optional viewport shading for the capture. Defaults to the current viewport; use MATERIAL or RENDERED to review materials and lighting instead of flat solid shading.

specs.name: capture_animation_playblast

contract.description: Render diagnostic close-up PNGs of named objects from bounded inspection views and expose them as MCP resources
contract.duration_hint: Synchronous still renders; rough estimate is a few seconds per object/view at 800x600 and more for heavy scenes or high resolutions.

timeout_recovery.message: If the MCP client times out, inspection renders may still finish. Wait, check bridge status, then read latest inspection-render metadata or audit logs before rerendering.

specs.description: Render diagnostic close-up PNGs of named objects from bounded inspection views, then return metadata plus MCP image resource URIs. Use when visual object details need rendered evidence, such as undersides, side views, occluded parts, or model defects. This is synchronous and may take a few seconds per object/view; if it times out, wait and check blender_bridge_status/latest inspection-render metadata before recapturing.

distance_factor.description: Camera distance as a multiple of target bounding radius. Defaults to 3.

frame.description: Frame to render. Defaults to the current frame.

lens.description: Temporary camera lens in mm. Defaults to 50.

note.description: Short reason stored in the render metadata.

object_names.description: Objects or root objects to render. Children are included when computing bounds.

views.description: Diagnostic view names. Defaults to front_below and side.

specs.name: capture_object_inspection_renders

contract.description: Return blend-file diagnostics for save path, dirty state, backups, missing external files, linked libraries, and data-block usage summaries

max_items.description: Maximum external file/library entries to return

specs.description: Inspect blend-file health: saved path, dirty state, backups, missing external files, linked libraries, and data-block usage summaries.

max_items.description: Maximum external file/library entries to return.

specs.name: get_blend_file_diagnostics

contract.description: Save the current .blend file, save-as to a new .blend path, or save a copy without changing the active file
contract.duration_hint: Usually seconds, but large .blend files or network paths can take longer. The current file path changes only when copy=false and filepath targets a different .blend.

copy.description: Save a copy without changing the active blend filepath. Requires filepath.

create_dirs.description: Create the target directory if missing. Defaults to true.

filepath.description: Optional .blend path. Omit to save the active file.

overwrite.description: Allow replacing an existing target .blend file.

user_confirmed_path.description: Required true when filepath is supplied for save-as/save-copy. Set only when the path came from the user or a file picker.

contract.path_policy: Saving the active bound .blend may omit filepath. Any save-as or save-copy filepath must come from the user and set user_confirmed_path=true.

timeout_recovery.message: If saving times out, wait, call blender_bridge_status, then check get_blend_file_diagnostics and the target file before retrying.

specs.description: Save the current .blend, save-as to a human-confirmed .blend path, or save a copy without changing the active file. Refuses accidental overwrite unless overwrite=true.

copy.description: Save a copy without changing the active filepath. Requires filepath.

create_dirs.description: Create the target directory if missing. Defaults to true.

filepath.description: Optional .blend path. Omit to save the active file.

overwrite.description: Allow replacing an existing target .blend file.

user_confirmed_path.description: Required true for save-as/save-copy. Set only after the user provides the filepath.

specs.name: save_blend_file

contract.description: Open an existing .blend file after explicit discard confirmation, creating a checkpoint first by default
contract.duration_hint: Usually seconds, but large .blend files can take longer. The active Blender session is replaced.

confirm_discard_current.description: Required true; opening replaces the active session.

create_checkpoint.description: Save a checkpoint of the current file before opening. Defaults to true.

load_ui.description: Load UI layout from the opened .blend when supported. Defaults to false.

require_checkpoint.description: Abort if checkpoint creation fails. Defaults to true.

user_confirmed_path.description: Required true. Set only when filepath came from the user or a file picker.

contract.path_policy: The filepath must come from the user or a file picker, and user_confirmed_path plus confirm_discard_current must both be true.

timeout_recovery.message: If opening times out, wait, call blender_bridge_status, then check get_blend_file_diagnostics before opening another file.

specs.description: Open an existing user-confirmed .blend file. This replaces the active Blender session, so confirm_discard_current and user_confirmed_path must be true; creates a checkpoint first by default.

confirm_discard_current.description: Required true; opening replaces the active session.

create_checkpoint.description: Save a checkpoint before opening. Defaults to true.

load_ui.description: Load UI layout from the opened file when supported. Defaults to false.

require_checkpoint.description: Abort if checkpoint creation fails. Defaults to true.

user_confirmed_path.description: Required true. Set only after the user provides the filepath.

specs.name: open_blend_file

contract.description: Create a new Blender project folder and .blend file after explicit discard confirmation, with optional standard subfolders
contract.duration_hint: Usually seconds. The active Blender session is replaced by a fresh startup scene and immediately saved.

confirm_discard_current.description: Required true; new project replaces the active session.

create_checkpoint.description: Save a checkpoint of the current file before creating the new project. Defaults to true.

create_dirs.description: Create the project directory if missing. Defaults to true.

create_standard_dirs.description: Create assets, refs, renders, and exports folders. Defaults to true.

overwrite.description: Allow replacing an existing target .blend file.

project_dir.description: Parent or final project directory. Required unless filepath is supplied.

project_name.description: Project name used for folder/filename when filepath is omitted.

require_checkpoint.description: Abort if checkpoint creation fails. Defaults to true.

template.description: Startup scene template. Defaults to default.

user_confirmed_path.description: Required true. Set only when project_dir or filepath came from the user or a file picker.

contract.path_policy: The project_dir or filepath must come from the user or a file picker, and user_confirmed_path plus confirm_discard_current must both be true.

timeout_recovery.message: If new-project creation times out, wait, call blender_bridge_status, then check get_blend_file_diagnostics and the target project folder before retrying.

specs.description: Create a new Blender project folder and .blend file at a user-confirmed path. This replaces the active Blender session, so confirm_discard_current and user_confirmed_path must be true; creates a checkpoint first by default.

confirm_discard_current.description: Required true; new project replaces the active session.

create_checkpoint.description: Save a checkpoint before creating the new project. Defaults to true.

create_dirs.description: Create the project directory if missing. Defaults to true.

create_standard_dirs.description: Create assets, refs, renders, and exports folders. Defaults to true.

overwrite.description: Allow replacing an existing target .blend file.

project_dir.description: Parent or final project directory. Required unless filepath is supplied.

project_name.description: Project name used for folder/filename when filepath is omitted.

require_checkpoint.description: Abort if checkpoint creation fails. Defaults to true.

user_confirmed_path.description: Required true. Set only after the user provides the project_dir or filepath.

specs.name: create_new_blender_project

contract.description: Autosave the current open .blend in place after Blender is already bound to a user-confirmed path
contract.duration_hint: Usually seconds. Autosave only saves when the current Blender session is already bound to a saved .blend path.

force.description: Save even if the interval has not elapsed, the file is not dirty, or live preview changes are pending. Defaults to false.

reason.description: Short reason stored in the result.

respect_enabled.description: When true, skip if the autosave preference is disabled. Defaults to false for manual calls.

contract.path_policy: No filepath is accepted. Autosave saves the active .blend in place and refuses unsaved scenes until the user provides a path through save/open/new.

timeout_recovery.message: If autosave times out, wait, call blender_bridge_status, then check get_blend_file_diagnostics and the active .blend file before retrying.

specs.description: Autosave the current open .blend in place. It has no filepath argument and refuses unsaved scenes.

force.description: Save even when the file is not dirty, the interval has not elapsed, or live preview changes are pending. Defaults to false.

respect_enabled.description: Skip if the autosave preference is disabled. Defaults to false.

specs.name: autosave_current_blend_file

contract.path_policy: Only relative paths contained by the directory of the current saved .blend are accepted. Absolute paths, parent traversal, hidden components, symbolic links, and reparse points are blocked.

specs.description: List files and folders beneath the directory containing the current saved .blend. Hidden paths and links are not traversed; unsaved projects are refused.

max_entries.description: Maximum entries to return, from 1 to 1000.

recursive.description: Recursively list ordinary subdirectories. Defaults to false.

relative_path.description: Optional relative project subdirectory. Absolute, parent, and hidden paths are rejected.

contract.path_policy: Only relative paths contained by the directory of the current saved .blend are accepted. Absolute paths, parent traversal, hidden components, symbolic links, and reparse points are blocked.

specs.description: Read a bounded UTF-8 or base64 file beneath the directory containing the current saved .blend. Reads are capped at 4 MiB and cannot follow hidden paths or links.

relative_path.description: Relative path beneath the saved .blend directory.

specs.name: read_project_file

contract.path_policy: Only relative paths contained by the directory of the current saved .blend are accepted. Absolute paths, parent traversal, hidden components, symbolic links, reparse points, executable or script targets, libraries, and .blend files are blocked.

specs.description: Create or replace a bounded UTF-8 or base64 file beneath the directory containing the current saved .blend. Parent folders may be created; executable/script/library and .blend targets are blocked.

content.description: UTF-8 text or base64 content. Decoded content is capped at 4 MiB.

create_dirs.description: Create ordinary parent directories. Defaults to true.

overwrite.description: Replace an existing file. Defaults to false.

relative_path.description: Relative output path beneath the saved .blend directory.

specs.name: write_project_file

contract.description: Start a long-running background Blender render job and return immediately with a job id, rough ETA, and status polling guidance. Auto quality keeps playblast/preview/review jobs low-res unless quality/resolution is specified.

timeout_recovery.message: If startup times out, check bridge status and latest render-job metadata before starting another job.

specs.description: Start a long-running render or playblast job in a background Blender process and return immediately with a job id, rough ETA, and polling guidance. Auto quality keeps final renders high quality but defaults playblast/preview/review/draft jobs to low resolution unless quality or resolution is specified. Use for 1080p/4K, high-sample, full-frame-range renders, frame sequences, or MP4 quality checks instead of blocking render tools or draft_script.

camera_name.description: Optional camera object to render from. Timeline camera markers still work when no camera is passed.

output_kind.description: Use frames for PNG sequences with progress counts, or video/mp4 for direct MP4 output.

quality.description: Defaulting profile when resolution/samples are omitted. Auto uses low-res for playblast/preview/review/draft jobs and final-quality defaults otherwise.

resolution_x.description: Output width. Defaults to auto quality profile: 640 for playblast/preview/review jobs, 1920 for final renders.

resolution_y.description: Output height. Defaults to auto quality profile: 360 for playblast/preview/review jobs, 1080 for final renders.

samples.description: Cycles/Eevee render samples. Defaults to auto quality profile: low for playblast/preview/review jobs, higher for final renders.
